failure_surface: Agent failed to execute any spreadsheet manipulation code, producing only syntax errors from malformed tool invocations and ultimately submitting an irrelevant trivial output.
root_cause: Malformed function call syntax prevented code execution, and the agent lacked error recovery strategies leading to abandonment of meaningful task implementation.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before submitting tool invocations, validate the XML/function call structure matches expected format; upon receiving syntax errors, pause to correct invocation syntax rather than repeating identical attempts; ensure actual spreadsheet processing logic executes before declaring completion.
evidence: Multiple consecutive SyntaxError entries showing malformed tool calls: <function=python>...\n</parameter> instead of proper closing tags
Four failed execution attempts all showing identical 'invalid syntax' pointing to tool call structure, not content code
No evidence of pandas/openpyxl imports, file loading, or spreadsheet manipulation logic in conversation history
Final solution was 'print(42)' which has no relation to the complex text pattern matching and sorting task requirements
Test case result of 0 indicates zero correctness metrics achieved despite instruction requiring specific column transformations

memory_items.title: Validate Tool Invocation Syntax Before Submission
memory_items.lesson: Function calls for spreadsheet tools must use correct XML/marker syntax; malformed closing tags prevent execution entirely.
memory_items.generalization: Always verify tool invocation structure conforms to expected format before submission; syntax errors block all downstream logic regardless of code quality.

memory_items.title: Implement Error Recovery on Execution Failures
memory_items.lesson: When code execution produces syntax or runtime errors, the agent should diagnose and correct the specific failure point rather than repeating identical calls.
memory_items.generalization: Upon encountering execution errors, analyze error type and source location; adjust approach based on error feedback before re-attempting execution.

memory_items.title: Ensure Substantial Task Logic Before Completion
memory_items.lesson: A valid solution must contain actual data processing logic addressing the task requirements, not placeholder or trivial code.
memory_items.generalization: Verify that submitted code implements meaningful spreadsheet operations (file loading, transformations, outputs) matching task specifications before considering execution complete.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Tool Invocation Hygiene
- Validate function call syntax before submission; malformed XML/marker tags will prevent all code execution.
- Verify opening and closing tags match the expected tool interface specification.

### Error Recovery Protocol
- Upon receiving syntax/runtime errors, stop and analyze the error source before re-executing.
- Do not repeat identical failing attempts; adjust code or invocation based on error feedback.

### Task Completion Verification
- Ensure submitted code contains substantive spreadsheet manipulation logic matching task requirements.
- Trivial outputs (e.g., print statements) do not satisfy data transformation tasks.
- Confirm actual file processing occurs (load, transform, save) before marking completion.
